markets.no_sub_title: Rain in NYC
markets.rules_primary: If the number of inches of precipitation recorded at Central Park, New York on March 12, 2026 is strictly greater than 0, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
The Payout Criterion only encompasses Expiration Values that are strictly greater than  inches. If the Expiration Value is T (when the target is 0) for Trace or R for Record (where the record set is strictly greater than ), then the market resolves to Yes.

This market will close and expire the sooner of the first 10:00 AM ET following the release of data for March 12, 2026 or March 19, 2026.

If the Climatological Report is still inconclusive on March 19, 2026, Kalshi will reference the columns titled "Weather" and "1 Hour Precip (in)" in the NWS time series (https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=knyc) for determination via rule 7.2 of the rulebook.
markets.title: Will it **rain** in New York City on Thursday?
markets.yes_sub_title: Rain in NYC

markets.no_sub_title: The Fate Of Ophelia
markets.rules_primary: If The Fate Of Ophelia is the #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 charts for Mar 28, 2026 then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
This market and these products have not been endorsed by Billboard. Any references to "Billboard", the "Hot 100," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between Billboard and Kalshi.The Billboard Hot 100 measures music consumption from a Friday to Thursday. This is released as the chart for the Saturday following Thursday (in this case, Mar 28, 2026).

Clarification 7/18/23: In the past, this market has used date to refer to the date at the end of the tracking week (the week in which Billboard records music consumption to be eligible for the Hot 100), a Thursday. In the future, the date in the Rulebook and the date listed in the short market rules on the market page will refer to the date that a given Billboard chart is dated for, as the Instructions in the market rules model. This is the second Saturday following the end of the tracking week. For example, today's new market will cover music consumption ending Thursday, July 20. The chart will be released on Tuesday, July 25. it will have a date corresponding to Saturday, July 29, 2023.
markets.subtitle: The Fate Of Ophelia:: Taylor Swift
markets.title: Will The Fate Of Ophelia be the #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 charts this week?
markets.yes_sub_title: The Fate Of Ophelia

Clarification 7/18/23: In the past, this market has used date to refer to the date at the end of the tracking week (the week in which Billboard records music consumption to be eligible for the Hot 100), a Thursday. In the future, the date in the Rulebook and the date listed in the short market rules on the market page will refer to the date that a given Billboard chart is dated for, as the Instructions in the market rules model. This is the second Saturday following the end of the tracking week. For example, today's new market will cover music consumption ending Thursday, July 20. The chart will be released on Tuesday, July 25. it will have a date corresponding to Saturday, July 29, 2023.
